ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest1.descripti: &nWelcome to Craft to Exile 2&r - a highly customized modpack that turns Minecraft into an action RPG.
The first chapter of quests, "Prologue" is meant to give you an overview to certain systems introduced in the modpack that may be different from what you're used to in most Minecraft modpacks.
&eComplete each "page" of the Prologue by clicking the checkmark.&r
For more information, visit our &9Wiki&r (you can also find the link in the main menu).
Also feel free to join our &9Discord&r server (link also on the main menu) where we can help you with any issues in game, or even just chat with you about the modpack!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest1.subtitle: Table of Contents
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest1.title: Welcome to Craft to Exile 2!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest10.descript: In Minecraft, &4Health&r is your life force, and if it hits 0, your character will die. In CTE2, you don't lose equipment on &4death&r, but starting at level 50, you will lose a good chunk of your experience.

Character defenses can be classified into 3 categories: &4Armor&r (plate), &2Dodge&r (leather), and &9Magic Shield&r (cloth), but may also consist of hybrid types (&8Brigandine&r = &4Armor&r/&2Dodge&r).

Armor reduces physical damage taken, dodge gives you a chance to completely negate any attack, and magic shield acts as an additional health buffer that regenerates slowly. You can improve these stats mainly through gear, stat points, and the Talent Tree.
There are &eother ways to mitigate damage&r, such as elemental resistances, spell dodge, and damage reduction. You can find these stats on gear, in the Talent Tree, and on Jewels.
There are options on the Talent Tree that may change how your defenses work, such as setting your health to 1 and giving a large bonus to magic shield!
&oIf you don't like how the armor looks, you can use Cosmetic Armor to cover it up!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest10.subtitle: Page 9
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest10.title: Suit Up
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest11.descript: Congratulations on completing the Prologue quest line. By completing this final page, you can finally &estart your journey in Act I&r!
In CTE2, &eyou can complete tasks in later quests even if you have yet to complete the dependencies&r. You won't be able to claim the rewards until you actually reach the quest, though.

Make sure to check out the &eSide Quests&r as they contain a lot of very useful information, and will introduce you to the other mods used in CTE2. There are various integrations between other mods and Mine and Slash that you can discover through these quest lines!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest11.subtitle: Appendix
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest11.title: Loot. Level. Explore.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest12.descript: Gems and Runes can be socketed into gear that have available sockets. You can add sockets through some Mine and Slash currencies.

&bGems&r come in multiple tiers. You can &ecombine 3 of them to create the next tier&r. Once you have socketed it into your item (click on the item with the Gem in your cursor). Once in your gear, the Gem will provide extra stats.

&cRunes&r can be added to gear to also provide extra stats. If you slot a specific combination of Runes in &eRuned gear&7, you can craft a &eRuneword&r which enhances the item with a specific set of bonuses! Note that the Runed gear can have more slots than the number of required Runes for the Runeword!
&eRight-click while holding a Rune to see available Runewords to craft!&r &eRunewords&r can only be created in &eRuned&r rarity items.
It is possible to retrieve Gems and Runes from items using an &eExtractor Currency Orb!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest12.subtitle: Page 9c
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest12.title: Gems and Runes
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest13.descript: Test out your new spells and armor in combat!
&eDo not summon the creature in the village as the Iron Golem may kill your Skeleton! Also be cautious of summoning it in daylight! Consider summoning in a cave.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest13.subtitle: Page 10
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest13.title: Easy Kill
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest14.descript: Well, in this case, money can be used to buy many useful tools and resources. You will be able to use money to barter for goods with villagers and traders from other mods. Of course, you can also use money to trade with other players, and if you're on a server, purchase claim blocks!
You can &learn money from killing mobs or looting chests&r. You can also sometimes sell goods for money with traders.
The &eTrading Terminal/Barter Board&r is a valuable tool in order to acquire specific resources, as well.&r
Keep your money safe in the &eWallet&r that you receive from this quest. Later on, upgrade the wallet so it can hold more money!
&eShould you need to exchange your money for lower amounts, you can use an ATM.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest14.subtitle: Page 1
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest14.title: Money
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest15.descript: In CTE2, you will craft and find many pieces of equipment. These pieces of &eequipment will not provide any stats unless you apply Gear Stat Souls to them&r, essentially imbuing them with Mine and Slash stats.
&cGear without a soul will have no effect, either defensively of offensively!&r

Gear Stat Souls can be used on any piece of equipment that shares the same type. For example, the Aquaculture and Farmer's Delight knives have the "Dagger" type. By &eclicking on the item with a Gear Stat Soul&r of "Dagger" type, you can apply Mine and Slash stats to the knives!
Want to learn more about how to &ecraft Gear Stat Souls&r? Visit the &aM\&S Professions&r quest chapter!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest15.subtitle: Page 5b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest15.title: Gear Stat Souls
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest16.descript: Shields (Armor), Totems (Dodge), and Tomes (Magic Shield) are offhands that can increase your defensive stat.
One defensive option in combat is the ability to roll (default: Alt). This is available to everyone. However, if you equip a shield, you can also use it to block damage.
Shields drain &aEnergy&r while being held up, and if you're facing the correct direction, negate damage taken.

Did you know that the &eshield can have a passive chance to negate damage&r (similar to dodging)? You can get block chance from gear or the Talent Tree, which allows you to have a &ochance to negate damage even when not holding the shield up&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest16.subtitle: Page 9b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest16.title: Blocking
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest17.descript: Waystones are spread throughout the world and once discovered, can be teleported to via other Waystones, Warp Scrolls, or Warp Stones.
Waystones can spawn randomly in the world, but are &eoften found in villages&r. Take some Warp Scrolls so you can easily return to any discovered Waystone!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest17.subtitle: Page 10c
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest17.title: Waystones
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest18.descript: You can &eclaim an area&r to be protected against unwanted players. Use any &eGolden Hoe&r to do this by right-clicking the ground.
&oCheck out the "&eLand Claiming with FLAN&r" quest under the &eJolly Cooperation&r chapter for more details!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest18.subtitle: Page 2c
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest18.title: Land Claiming
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest19.descript: Throughout your travels, you will find &eSpirit Orbs&r, which can be exchanged at &eGoddess Statues&r for &cHeart Containers&r. Each Heart Container increases your base Health by 2, up to a maximum of 200 Health (100 Heart Containers). You can also find &eHeart Containers from Bosses&r in Craft to Exile 2.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest19.subtitle: Page 11
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest19.title: Heart Containers
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest2.descripti: CTE2 supports party questing and play. &eAll Teams/Party option are accessible through the Inventory!&r
There are 3 different commands to use for party play:
&eMine and Slash's teams&r, allows you to share EXP and heal/buff each other. Additionally, you will see party member's Health/Magic Shield values in the GUI!
FTB Teams allows you to share quest progression. FTB Teams also has a custom team chat menu, press - by default to access it.
Lightman's Teams allows you to share money and trader notifications.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest2.subtitle: Page 2b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest2.title: Party Play
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest20.descript: You will very soon realize that &eCraft to Exile 2&r has a LOT of loot to collect. Most of the loot comes from &eMine and Slash&r, the RPG mod. One of the most frustrating things is keeping your inventory organized and having enough space to pick things up.

This &eMaster Bag&r will specifically &bpick up and hold items&r from &eMine and Slash&r and assist you with organizing your loot. You can wear the bag in a Curio slot and access it via "&eAlt + B&r"!
If you are still struggling with the sheer quantity of loot, you could also consider using the &eauto-salvage feature&r found in the Hub. To learn more about this, visit the &eProfessions Chapter&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest20.subtitle: Page 11b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest20.title: Managing Your Inventory
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest21.descript: &eCraft to Exile 2&r comes with &ecustom key binds&r. However, there have been &ccases where the custom key binds did not apply&r. You can confirm this by pressing R. If it brings up the wheel, then the custom key binds did not apply (it should be H).
To fix this, hit Esc and go to &bOptions > Controls > Key Binds > click Reset Keys&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest21.subtitle: Page 1b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest21.title: Key Binds
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest22.descript: In CTE2 there is a limit on how many mobs can be killed within a chunk before they &cstop yielding any drops or experience!&r The chunk will recover over time.
This is done to encourage travel and exploration instead of the usage of automated experience methods.
&oExplore and discover! There are many structures and dangers to be found.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest22.subtitle: Page 10b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest22.title: Anti Mob Farm
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest23.descript: Craft to Exile 2 comes bundled with &eVR support&r! You &lmust&r have the following:
- Minecraft Window is Focused on PC

Once the above are checked, turn on your headset and connect it to your PC. Then you can enable VR from the main menu. Some key binds are already set up for you. However, you will probably want to &econfigure SteamVR inputs&r for Vivecraft to better suit your needs.

By default, the &lVR radial menu&r is used for Mine and Slash Skill hotkeys and some other important menu items. When bringing up the radial menu, you can hold the &lGrip button to access the second set of options&r!

Additionally, there is VR integration support with most aspects of Minecraft, such as the Crafting Table and immersive swinging and bow usage! You can also modify locomotion and enable run in place to move!
&lPerform the following to prevent the GUI from failing to load. Before exiting the game, you need turn off VR in the main menu and subsequently, re-enable it on log in.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest23.title: VR Support
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest24.descript: &eSkills&r can be assigned to your hotbar by clicking &eSkill Hotbar&r from the hub.

&eYou can move the Skill hotbar&r by pressing ESC > Mods > Mine and Slash > Config > mine_and_slash-client > General > Overlays > &eSet SPELL HOTBAR OVERLAY TYPE to toggle between horizontal/vertical and SPELL HOTBAR VERTICAL/HORIZONTAL CONFIGs to adjust&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest24.subtitle: Page 8b
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest24.title: The Spell Bar
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest25.descript: CTE2 has tons of information, and sometimes it can be a bit spread across the campaign. If you're looking for this information consolidated in one place, you can use the Patchoulie &eAdventurer Handbook&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest25.subtitle: Page 11c
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest25.title: Information Compiled
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest3.descripti: Thanks to Mine and Slash, CTE2 has a fully customized leveling progression system.

Kill mobs to earn experience, and receive stat points with each level. You can allocate these points in the main hub by &epressing "H"&r. In fact, most of the menus will be accessible from this menu wheel!
Your player stats can also be increased via other means, such as: equipment (gear) and talent points which we'll cover later.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest3.subtitle: Page 2
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest3.title: Character Stats
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest4.descripti: The &eTalent Tree&r allows for an additional layer of &echaracter customization&r, and is found in the Mine and Slash hub.

Each level grants you &e1 talent point.&r You will need to choose your starting position, and then &eallocate points into nodes&r connected to any other nodes you already have. Each &enode&r provides &ebonuses&r to stats, and in some cases, can alter your core gameplay loop!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest4.subtitle: Page 3
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest4.title: Talent Tree
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest5.descripti: The &eCharacters&r menu lets you save and load different characters. It will save all of your character's information except for their gear! You can have up to 3 saved characters. Newly created characters start at level 1 but share quest completion unlocks such as &eTalent and Ascendancy points&r.
The &eSalvaging&r menu lets you turn bad loot into useful crafting materials upon pick-up.
The &eClasses&r menu allows character specialization. You will learn skills here and can improve them with points. In CTE2, you can &especialize in two different classes&r!
The &eJewels&r menu is used once you've allocated any points into Jewel Sockets in the Talent Tree. Equipping Jewels can improve your character stats!
Additionally, at the bottom of the GUI (where the pickaxes are), you can see your &eProfession&r levels.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest5.subtitle: Page 4
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest5.title: The Rest of the GUI
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest6.descripti: In CTE2, there are 3 "main" fighting styles. You can either be a &4close-ranged warrior&r, a &9magic spellcaster&r, or a &2long-ranged archer&r. This is of course, a basic generalization, as each character can be customized and expressed extensively.
Do you want to be a flame-spewing warrior? A necromantic spellcaster? Or even a trap throwing hunter?
CTE2 has a lot to offer in terms of character customization. Hopefully, you will find the playstyle that best suits you!
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest6.subtitle: Page 5
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest6.title: Your First Weapon
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest7.descripti: Combat has been overhauled in Craft to Exile 2. &eWeapons have animations and combos&r, and have different properties depending on the type of weapon you wield.
Additionally, you can press &e"Alt" to dodge roll&r, which has a short cooldown.
Don't forget about your basic attacks in CTE2! They are powerful in combat and can be used in conjunction with skills.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest7.subtitle: Page 6
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest7.title: Combat
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest8.descripti: Skills require &9Mana&r or &aEnergy&r and are used in combat. They can be learnt through the Classes tab and can be equipped in the &9square&r slots in the Skills GUI:
You can modify and enhance Skills by either leveling them, or linking them to Support Gems, which go in the &5circular&r slots. These can increase the &eResource Cost&r and power of the linked Skill.

Skills have a maximum number of Support Gems that can be linked (see "&5Support Slots:&r"). The number of sockets a Skill can have is determined by the &eSkill's level&r, with a max of 5 sockets at level 16. &eTo get the first slot, your skill must be at least level 4.&r &cThe number of slots is &lalso&r limited by your &eplayer level&r (1 slot every 10 levels, starting at 1).&r
In the &ebottom&r row, equip Augment Gems which use up Augment Capacity to provide stats. You can see how much Augment Capacity you have remaining in &dpink&r to the right.
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest8.subtitle: Page 8
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest8.title: Skill, Support, and Augment Gems
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest9.descripti: Test your new blade and knowledge in combat!
&eDo not summon the creature in the village as the Iron Golem may kill your Zombie! Also be cautious of summoning it in daylight! Consider summoning in a cave.&r
&oMake sure to allocate your stats before the fight, as weapons have a minimum stat requirement!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest9.subtitle: Page 7
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.quest9.title: First Blood
ftbquests.chapter.act_0.title: Prologue
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest1.descripti: Scouting reports indicate a rise in Illager activity. There have even been reports of increased necromantic activities!
Check the area for any undead creatures, and &ekill them&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest1.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest1.title: Training Arc
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest10.descript: The &eInquisitor&r is the right-hand Illager of the Invoker. Taking one out will deal a heavy blow to the Invoker's forces.
You can find the Inquisitor in the &eIllager Fortress&r. They can also be encountered through forced &eGateway of the Raiders&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest10.subtitle: Illager Arc
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest10.title: Illager Advisor I
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest11.descript: You've defeated the Illager Invoker... But the Overworld is not safe yet.

Due to the activities of the Illagers, rifts to other dimensions have opened in the Overworld. You'll need to go into each dimension and stabilize the inter-dimensional connection. Otherwise, the Overworld may eventually be overrun with other-worldly creatures!

It's time to go to the &eNether&r - a hellish landscape of flame. &eYou can get there by setting up an Obsidian portal. Activate the portal using Flint and Steel, and you'll be able to enter the Nether.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest11.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest11.title: Realm Unexplored
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest12.descript: CTE2's Campaign quest line is split into multiple acts, where each act is associated with a different dimension, preceded by a Prologue quest line (which you're already familiar with).

As you complete the main quests, more side quests will unravel. And once you've completed the line of main quests (typically 5-7 quests in length), you will receive the quest to move on to the next act/dimension.

As you move from one dimension to the next, the mobs will become stronger, and the loot quality will improve. Although mobs scale to player level, they cap out in each dimension, making it possible to "overlevel" for a dimension if you're struggling with it.
&6This Quest Unlocks New Shops In the Campaign Shop.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest12.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest12.title: Acts
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest13.descript: Luminescent creatures lurk in the underground to attract attention. Find some &eGlow Skeletons&r, kill them, and continue to explore the depths.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest13.subtitle: The Underground
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest13.title: Glow in the Dark Issues
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest14.descript: Why are the skeletons glowing in the first place? The creatures from which skeletons arise from weren't glowing in the dark...
To investigate this potential osteo-mutation, find a &eHermit Abysswalker&r. You hear they can be found in through &eGateway of Overworldian Nights&r.
Be careful, it can cast various spells and summon minions to aid it in combat.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest14.subtitle: The Underground
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest14.title: Mutagenic Source
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest15.descript: You've heard numerous tales of underground &eGoblins&r and &eKobolds.&r Some tales say these creatures are aggressive, and not to be trifled with. But others say Kobolds and Goblins are the friendliest underground creatures... Provided you &ebring a bit of coin&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest15.subtitle: The Underground
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest15.title: Hidden Traders
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest16.descript: &eXP Blocks&r are blocks that can be crafted and placed. They let you store any &evanilla experience&r into the block.
- Sneak + right-click with an empty hand to add all your current experience to the block.
- Right-click to remove one level from the block and add it to your experience bar.
&cNote that your offhand must be empty!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest16.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest17.descript: In CTE2, there are various custom weapon types. Below is a list of all the weapon types, with the custom ones highlighted in &eyellow&r.
Sword, Axe, Bow, Crossbow, &eStaff, Dagger, Spear, Hammer, Greatsword, Scythe, Gauntlet&r.
Keep in mind you will need Gear Stat Souls when crafting these to give them Mine and Slash stats.
In CTE2 you can also equip &etwo-handed weapons&r. At the moment, &eSpears, Greatswords, and Scythes&r are two-handed.
You can also &edual-wield any one-handed weapons&r! Weapons in the off-hand provide &e25%%&r of their stats.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest17.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest17.title: Other Weapon Types, Two-Handers, Dual-Wielding
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest18.descript: In the &eCampaign&r quest line, various quests will have a different background shape which can indicate what type of quest it is.
Additionally, you will find &e"Lost Pages" which have the paper icon&r. These are simple click to complete quests that provide additional information.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest18.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest18.title: Quest Shapes
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest19.descript: Throughout your journey, you will find yourself looking for more powerful gear. Gear power can be assessed in 3 main ways:
1. The &elevel&r of the gear, which determines the power of the gear's stats. Gear level is typically determined by the level of the mob that is killed.

2. The &erarity&r of the gear, which determines how many and how high the stat can roll, and how many sockets it can have. There are 7 droppable rarities: &7Common&r, &aUncommon&r, &9Rare&r, &dEpic&r, &4Legendary&r, &5Mythic&r, and &cUnique&r. Unique gear is special because the stats rolled on them are predetermined. They are powerful and can sometimes enable special styles of play.
3. The &equality&r of the gear, which further improves the power slightly.

Some of these attributes can be changed via Mine and Slash &ecurrencies&r, such as the Orb of Infinity which can increase a gear's level. Additionally, some currencies can be crafted through the Mine and Slash profession system to further enhance your gear.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest19.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest19.title: Gear Improvement
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest2.descripti: You've found a &eZombie Gateway&r from the last creatures you fought. Oddly, you sense an uncomfortable energy emanating from it.
Could it be the cause for the uprise in undead creatures? It's worth taking a look. Try using the pearl away from a village. It seems like it might open a portal upon use...
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest2.subtitle: Level Methods
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest2.title: Disturbances in the Air
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest20.descript: Favor is a system that rewards players for living. Every minute, you generate a small amount of Favor. &eFavor soft-caps at 1000&r. However, you can go beyond this Favor limit by opening chests.
At 250+ Favor, you will have a 2%% bonus to loot drops. As you go up in Favor, your bonus to loot drops will increase.
At the &ecap of 2500&r you have a 25%% increase to loot and experience!
Note that there are level requirements for certain rarities to start appearing:
Be careful though, &cdying will cause you to lose 500 Favor!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest20.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest20.title: Favor
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest21.descript: Read all of the &eLost Pages&r of Act I to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest21.subtitle: Achievement
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest21.title: Read All Act I Lost Pages!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest22.descript: &eComforts&r adds &esleepings bags&r that can be used while adventuring. Instead of bringing a bulky bed with you, just plop the sleeping bag down and &erest!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest22.subtitle: Comforts
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest23.descript: Hold down &e`&r to activate &eUltiminer,&r which is an advanced form of &eveinmining.&r An outline will appear showing the blocks that will be mined. Hold down shift + ` to select a &epattern,&r such as Mining Tunnel which mines a staircase. Only the &eShapeless pattern will veinmine&r, the rest adhere to their pattern.
Using Ultiminer will take &eextra durability and hunger&r and also requires the appropriate tool.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest23.subtitle: Efficiency
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest23.title: FTB Ultimine
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest24.descript: The &eExplorer's Compass&r allows you to locate &estructures&r while the &eNature's Compass&r allows you to find &especific biomes&r. You may need to change location to find what you're looking for.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest24.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest25.descript: &eBamboo Spikes&r will hurt anything that steps on it. You can also apply &epotion effects&r to them.
The &eFlute&r can be played to attract any &etamed mobs&r that may be lost. Right click a mob with it to link the flute with that pet.
Use the &eCage&r to &egrab&r mobs, particularly tamed mobs, to transport them.
&eNotice Boards&r can display written books' text.

&eSupplementary Jars&r can store 4 buckets or &e12&r bottles of any vanilla liquids (like honey, milk, lava, potions, soups, dragon breath or xp) as well as and fireflies. Jars can also store small mobs like slimes and bees. To &ecatch&r vexes and endermites you'll have to use tinted ones. &eShift right click&r to place a mob jar instead of releasing its mob.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest25.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest25.title: Supplementaries' Utilities
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest26.descript: Seems this &e"Chemical X"&r drives evolutionary mutations wild. &ePigs&r are simple and weak, they must be an easy and &esafe&r animal to test this stuff on. Feed a Pig a &eFermented Spider Eye&r and throw &eChemical X&r at it. This process has a chance to fail, more &eChemical X&r may be purchased on the &eTrading Terminal/Barter Board&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest26.subtitle: Your Own Lab
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest26.title: How Bad Could It Be?
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest27.descript: All this running around has to be tiring... Maybe it's time for a &emount.&r Craft the &eChocopedia&r to learn about Chocobo &ebreeding&r and &etaming.&r You can even breed special Chocobos that have the ability to &efly!&r

&eTransport&r mounts and pets of any kind through &eWaystones&r by putting them into &eCages&r or by having them on a &eLead.&r Even without a Cage or Lead, they can teleport with you through the Waystone!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest27.subtitle: Wild Chocobos
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest27.title: Chococraft and Mounts
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest28.descript: Potions from the Alchemy Profession can provide useful benefits such as healing and buffs. You can see how to craft these potions in more detail in the "&aM\&S Professions&r" chapter. Alternatively, if you're not into Professions, &eyou can also buy these potions&r from various traders (Villagers, Goblin Traders).
&l&eTo quick-use potions, you can press Y!&r&r
Brew the &aUncommon Health Potions&r with the following:
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest28.subtitle: M\&S Professions
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest28.title: Taking On Work
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest29.descript: &eWaystones&r allow you to teleport to and from each one you've unlocked. Global Waystones are usable across dimensions.
The &eWarp Stone&r allows you to teleport to any waystone you've unlocked but has a cooldown.
There are also &enumerous scrolls&r to craft that can act as &esingle use Warp Stones&r or to teleport to a &especific Waystone&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest29.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest29.title: Warping Around
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest3.descripti: The scouts have come back with another report! Obscenely large Zombies, Skeletons, and Creepers have been sighted around the Overworld.
This can only be the work of the &eWitches&r. You'll need to find a Witch, interrogate her, and take any suspicious items.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest3.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest3.title: A Brewsome Problem
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest30.descript: In Craft to Exile 2, you can find &eDimension-specific Runes&r that can be used to slot into your gear, or craft powerful Runewords.
&eSid Rune&r - Warden (&b10%%&r), Night Lich (&b15%%&r), Nether Gauntlet (&b15%%&r), Void Blossom (&b15%%&r), Obsidilith (&b15%%&r)
There are &e7 of these Runes&r in total, and they can only be obtained from Dimension Bosses such as the Invoker or End Dragon.
&eOwd Rune&r - Hermit Abysswalker (&b5%%&r), Invoker (&b10%%&r), Frostmaw (&b15%%&r), Grim Reaper (&b10%%&r)
&eNet Rune&r - Warped Mosco (&b10%%&r), Wildfire (&b5%%&r), Mother Lava Squid (&b5%%&r), Wither (&b10%%&r), Awful Ghast (&b15%%&r), Nether Keeper (&b15%%&r)

&eUnd Rune&r - Ferrous Wroughtnaut (&b10%%&r), Umvuthi (&b10%%&r), Tongbi, the Sculptor (&b10%%&r), Forgotten Guardian (&b10%%&r), Eel (&b15%%&r), Mother of the Maze (&b50%%&r), Ghost of Captain Cornelia (&b10%%&r)
&eEnd Rune&r - Ender Dragon (&b7.5%%&r), Void Worm (&b5%%&r), Shelterer (&b15%%&r)
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest30.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest30.title: Dimension Runes
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest31.descript: &eParagliders&r can be used to easily traverse large open distances - assuming you start from a high enough starting point. You can use source of fire to create ascending winds, allowing you to float upwards.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest31.subtitle: Traversing Space
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest31.title: Paragliding
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest32.descript: &eTransmogrification Tables&r can be crafted and used to change the appearance of your items.
Place the table in the world and right-click it. From left to right, the slots are:
1. &eThe Fuel Slot&r - fueled by Amethyst Shards.
2. &eThe Target Slot&r - the item you place here will have its appearance changed.
3. &eThe Appearance Slot&r - the item you place here will have its appearance copied onto the target. The item here will NOT be consumed upon transmogrification!
Additionally, you can place &eVoid Fragments&r into the Appearance Slot to make the item hidden!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest32.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest32.title: Looking Cooler
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest33.descript: Are you playing as a &5Warlock&r, &2Hunter&r, or &9Elementalist&r? Did you know these classes have access to summons?

You can configure your &esummon behaviour&r in the &eFeatures&r tab of the Mine and Slash Hub (bottom left). You can make them aggressive, or passive. If set to passive, the summons will only attack what you've attacked.
Additionally, you can &eunsummon&r your summons at any time using the unsummon keybind (Default: &eDelete&r)!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest33.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest33.title: Summoning Classes
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest34.descript: &eSpice of Life: Onion Edition &rgives every food a &e"diversity"&r factor. Eating diverse foods gives &ebenefits,&r while sticking to a few foods won't. &eCheck your Food Book in your MineMenu (default: H)&r to check your diversity and current benefits. &eKeep eating different foods and increase your Health, Block Reach, Movement Speed, and more!&r
The &eLunchbag&r can be crafted and upgraded to &ehold all your food&r at once by &ecrouch + right-clicking&r with it in hand and right clicking to eat with it.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest34.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest34.title: Spice of Life: Onion Edition
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest35.descript: &eRestless Spirits&r are wrathful spirits that spawn at night. They can be found anywhere.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest35.subtitle: The Graveyard
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest35.title: Bestiary II
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest36.descript: The &eSpirit Guides&r can be found in &edry and hot biomes&r. They don't burn in the sun due to their hat. During battle, it will call on allies to assist.
&cThe Spirit Guide spawns at minimum level 10.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest36.subtitle: Lv. 10 - Spiritual Guidance
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest36.title: Bestiary III
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest37.descript: The &eDoor Knight&r and &eSkeleton Thrasher&r are defensive undead mobs. They can spawn anywhere. To gain completion for these tasks, you must find wild ones.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest37.subtitle: Undead Guardians
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest37.title: Bestiary IV
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest38.descript: The &eMissionary&r is a deceptively powerful undead creature. You can find them anywhere. If engaged, it will start spawning undead creatures to assist it.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest38.subtitle: Mission Five
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest38.title: Bestiary V
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest39.descript: &e&oRequires the completion of The End in Act IV.&r&r
The &eFrostmaw&r is an ancient creature found in snowy biomes. Their massive size, impressive speed, and powerful ice breath make them near-impossible opponents.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest39.subtitle: Lv. 50 - Frigid Wastelands
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest39.title: Bestiary VII
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest4.descripti: It seems like the Witch was brewing up a suspicious potion. It's labelled &e"Chemical X"&r. Maybe you should see what it does, it might be responsible for the large creatures.
Try tossing this onto a &eregular skeleton&r and seeing what happens. Or if I'm unlucky enough, I'll run into a &eMutant Skeleton&r on my own.
&eNote that if you encounter a Mutant Zombie, it will revive up to 4 times unless it dies while burning!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest4.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest4.title: An Experiment Gone Wrong
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest40.descript: The &eSpiders Mother&r is a large predatory spider found in &eCaves&r. They can summon smaller spiders to aid in combat.
&e&oThe Bestiary books will unlock as you progress through the Acts.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest40.subtitle: Putrid Grounds
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest40.title: Bestiary I
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest41.descript: You can craft gear to suit up for your adventure. Check out the M\&S Professions Chapter for in-depth information!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest41.subtitle: M\&S Professions
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest41.title: Suit Up II
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest42.descript: &eRepair Kits&r automatically repair equipped gear and tools every 10 seconds. The amount they repair depends on the tier of the kit.
They have limited uses, denoted by their charge. You can fill up a kit by feeding it &eMine and Slash Stones&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest42.subtitle: Gear Retention
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest42.title: Auto-Repair
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest43.descript: &eSir Pumpkinhead&r is a mysterious spirit found during the month of October... However, you can also summon him by destroying a &especial pumpkin&r surrounded by candles that you can find in the world.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest43.subtitle: Pumpking
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest43.title: Bestiary VI
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest44.descript: &eLord Pumpkinhead&r is a stronger and final version of &eSir Pumpkinhead&r. You can fight him by summoning him. Right-click on a &eInfernal Evil Pumpkin&r (created or found) using a &eTransmuting Elixir&r.
Note that one of the components for the &eTransmuting Elixir&r, the &eChaos Component&r can be made by brewing a &eSeed of Chaos&r in a &eBottle of Water&r using a &eBrewing Stand&r.
&cLord Pumpkinhead spawns at minimum level 60 and Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest44.subtitle: Lv. 60 - Pumpking II
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest44.title: Bestiary VIb
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest45.descript: &eZombie Clowns&r are slow and dense enemies. They apply Intoxicating Brew on hit. They can be found in &eClown Caravans&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest45.subtitle: Joke On You!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest45.title: Bestiary IIb
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest46.descript: You can craft or find your own &eWaystone&r to teleport to. These will be relatively expensive, but they're definitely worth it!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest46.subtitle: The Extras
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest47.descript: The &eDire Hound Leader&r is a large Hound found wandering around all different biomes at &bNight&r. They can summon smaller Dire Hounds to aid in combat.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest47.subtitle: Howling Hounds
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest47.title: Bestiary Ib
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest48.descript: The &eFallen Chaos Knight&r a proud knight that has succumbed to chaos. They can spawn at &bNight&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest48.subtitle: Fallen Knights
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest48.title: Bestiary IVb
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest49.descript: During your travels, you may find &eObelisk Maps&r in chests. These can be used on &eObeslisks&r to trigger wave-based instanced encounters!
The mobs that spawn are affected by modifiers on the Map itself. These modifiers will make the encounter more difficult, but increase rewards.
Mobs that spawn in Obelisk encounters scale to your level.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest49.subtitle: Wave-Based Instances
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest5.descripti: You've confirmed that the Witches were brewing potions of Chemical X to be used on Overworld creatures, turning them into hideous monstrosities.
However, there must be some reason they've all collectively started brewing Chemical X... Perhaps it's time to do the scouting yourself. Your intuition points towards the dastardly &eIllagers.&r

Maybe you can scout out what the Illagers are up to. They've been known to outsource their potion-creation to Witches in the past. You can find &ePillagers&r in &eVanilla Pillager Outposts&r which spawn frequently in the Overworld.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest5.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest5.title: Investigation
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest50.descript: &eBounty Hunting&r is a Mine and Slash Profession which levels up as you complete Bountiful bounties. Complete bounties to earn Bounty Hunting experience and &eBounty Slips&r!
&eBounty Boards&r will generate &erandom bounties&r for you to complete. Take out a &erequest&r from the board's interface and complete it within the &etime limit&r to earn the listed rewards!
You can either craft the Bounty Boards or find them randomly throughout the world - generally in villages!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest50.subtitle: M\&S Professions
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest50.title: Bountiful Boards
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest51.descript: Trims provide modifiers if used on your armor. There are different trims to be found and each can provide different benefits. You will need a &eSmithing Table&r to apply a trim.
&eKobold Smithing Templates&r can provide the following bonuses to armor, for example:
&e#Helmet&r: more vanilla experience from killing mobs.
&e#Chestplate&r: faster block breaking speed when using an incorrect or no tool.
&e#Leggings&r: slower hunger drain when performing actions.
&e#Boots&r: farmland can no longer be trampled.
Find other trims and customize your gear!
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest51.subtitle: The Underground
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest51.title: Free Trimming
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest52.descript: Mobs in Craft to Exile have stats, and some mobs will always spawn with specific stats in addition to the randomly generated stats. Certain quests will sometimes mention what stats the associated mob will spawn with. For example, You'll notice in Act II that most mobs mentioned in quests have some sort of &cFire&r modifier.
You can also view the exact stats of a mob you are looking at by pressing "&e=&r".
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest52.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest52.title: Monster Stats
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest6.descripti: These &eIllagers&r have been up to no good. They've been raiding villages and are found commonly in &eOverworld structures&r.
Now that you know who's behind the rise in hostile creatures, it's time to &ebring the fight&r to them.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest6.subtitle: Illager Arc
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest6.title: Spread Prevention
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest7.descripti: These Illagers can only be easily found through forced &eGateway of the Raiders&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest7.subtitle: Illager Arc
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest7.title: Raiding Party
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest8.descripti: Looks like your suspicions were warranted. Through interrogation, you've learnt that the Illager &eInvoker&r is the mastermind behind these evil schemes.

Track down the Invoker and put an end to his evil plans. You've been told they can be found in &eWoodland Mansions&r... They can also apparently be encountered through forced &eGateway of the Raiders&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest8.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest8.title: The Mastermind
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest9.descripti: The &eIllusioner&r is deceptive and cunning. It's no surprise that they're the Invoker's left-hand Illager. You can find them in &eIllusioner Towers&r. They can also be encountered through forced &eGateway of the Raiders&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest9.subtitle: Illager Arc
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.quest9.title: Illager Advisor II
ftbquests.chapter.act_i.title: Act I
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest1.descript: Well these &eLemurians&r certainly don't belong here...
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest1.subtitle: Risk of Rain
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest1.title: Otherworldly Beings II
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest10.descrip: You've reported your achievements to the village guard and received your rewards. It's time to move onto the next dimension - the &eUndergarden&r. A moist, sticky, and mostly habitable dimension with some curious creatures... They might not be mutated, but it's worth taking a look anyway.
Craft a &eCatalyst&r using your &eNether Star&r, and access the Undergarden by making a portal with &eStone Bricks in the same shape as the Nether Portal&r. Activate it using the Catalyst.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest10.title: Travel Catalyst
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest11.descrip: You've seen in Nether Fortresses clues to another structure. "The Citadel" is etched into the walls of the Fortress in various locations. Maybe you can find the &eCitadel&r and investigate.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest11.title: A Castle in the Dark
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest12.descrip: If you find yourself dying frequently, you can craft a &eRespawn Anchor&r. To craft one, you will need to first find some &eCrying Obsidian&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest12.title: Dried Up Tears
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest13.descrip: Occasionally, you'll see large, looming structures in The Nether. How is that possible? Is there civilization here?
Maybe finding one of these structures and exploring it will provide you clues on where to find powerful creatures.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest13.title: Nether Fortress
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest14.descrip: If the &eCrimson Mosquito&r drinks the blood of a &eMungus&r which is coated in &eWarped Fungus&r, then the mosquito will turn blue and start to grow in size - eventually turning into a &eWarped Mosco&r.
Alternatively, you can feed a &eCrimson Mosquito with Warped Mixture&r. Unsure where to get that? You can &epurchase it from the Trading Terminal/Barter Board&r!
That sounds like a very mutagenic process...
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest14.title: Forest Chad
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest15.descrip: &eGear Soul Extractors&r can be used on gear. You will get the "stats" of the gear back as a soul, destroying the base item in the process. You can then use the soul on another piece of gear!
Conversely, &eGear Soul Cleaners&r are used to get rid of the stats of the gear while keeping the base item.

&eGem Extractors&r are used to remove &eGems&r from your socketed gear. There is no penalty for doing so, but the extractor will be consumed. There is also a &eRune Extractor&r, but using that will cost &e1 Gear Potential&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest15.title: Gear Soul Extractors
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest16.descrip: The &eNether Keeper&r is a powerful foe found in the &eKeeping Castle&r of The Nether. Keeping Castles can be found in &eNether Wastes and Crimson Forests underground&r. You can summon it by placing a &eNether Star in the altar&r.
&cNote that these structures cannot be found conventionally with the Explorer's Compass. You may need to find them manually.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest16.title: Defenders of the Nether II
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest17.descrip: Defeat all of the mini-bosses of Act II to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your hot new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest17.title: Defeat All Act II Mini-Bosses!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest18.descrip: Magnets are incredibly useful for dungeons and exploring as they will siphon any dropped items towards you. Hold one in hand and crouch + right-click to configure its range and filters. Can even be upgraded to have a far larger range!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest18.title: How Do They Work?
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest19.descrip: &eLightman's Currency&r is a mod that adds those coins you get for killing things. You can use these coins to sometimes trade with villagers, purchase Support and Augment gems at the end of each Act, or set up shops and trade with other players.

Even in singleplayer, you can purchase items from the trader. Craft to Exile 2 has some default items that can be bought regardless of world. Set up a &eTrading Terminal/Barter Board&r to check it out!
Examples of things you can buy:
If you're playing in multiplayer, you can even purchase &eFLAN claim blocks&r using the FLAN buy option.
&oFor more information, refer to "&eLightman's Currency&r" quest in the &eJolly Cooperation&r chapter!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest19.title: Buying Happiness
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest2.descript: Mosquitoes are annoying enough in the Overworld... These things are huge, they stick to your face, and are generally difficult to kill!
These hideous, mutant flying insects are found in the &eforests of The Nether&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest2.subtitle: Crimson Forests
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest2.title: Bloody Pests
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest20.descrip: Craft a &eTarget Dummy&r and place it down. You can hit it to view your damage per second! You can also enable the &5Damage Chat-Log&r option in the &eFeatures&r tab of the &eMNS Hub&r to view &eexact damage value breakdowns&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest21.descrip: &eCurrencies&r are various items that can be used directly on your Gear. They will modify your Gear and empower them.

Each Gear comes with a set amount of &ePotential&r. Using a Currency on the Gear will reduce the Gear's &ePotential&r. Once the Potential has reached 0, Currency with Potential Cost may no longer be applied to it. Not all Currencies use Potential, however, and may be used at any time.

Certain Currencies, such as the &eOrb of Chaos,&r can add very powerful effects, but may cause negative effects to your Gear. &cWith the Orb of Chaos, there is a 25%% chance that your item will turn into Coal&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest21.title: More On Currencies
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest22.descrip: You may have noticed some &eNether&r mobs dealing more &cFire Damage.&r Mobs native to a specific dimension tend to have an affinity towards a specific element. For example, in the Nether, some mobs will deal a combination of Fire and Physical Damage.
&oAlso note, this doesn't mean they will take less damage from the element they have an affinity for.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest22.title: Mob Affinities
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest23.descrip: Mine and Slash Professions can sometimes be tedious to level... Did you know that gaining experience through combat gives you &eRested Profession Experience&r? This experience pools and will be consumed when performing Profession activities to enhance your experience gained.
Similarly, performing Profession activities will give you &eRested Combat Experience&r!
You can view how much Rested Experience you have in the Mine and Slash Hub by mousing over the Profession icon.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest23.title: A Moment's Respite
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest24.descrip: &eAscendancy&r is a way to further grow your character by completing challenging tasks. To start, you must &ecomplete the "Defenders of the Nether" quest&r, which will unlock the &eAscendancy Chapter&r.
The first quest you complete will earn you 1 Ascendancy point, just enough to choose your specialization. With each subsequent challenge you complete, you will earn more Ascendancy points!
These points are capped at 7, and you can use these points similar to Talent points in the Ascendancy tab of the &eMine and Slash Hub&r.
You can only select one Ascendancy tree at a time. To select another tree, you must first respec and retrieve all of your points in your current tree.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest24.title: Ascendancy
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest25.descrip: Craft a hearty meal to keep yourself alive! Eating foods will give you bonus stats for a set duration.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest25.title: Food For Thought
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest26.descrip: Try your hand at crafting a custom weapon to help with your adventure!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest26.title: The Sharpest
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest27.descrip: &ePhantom Creepers&r spawn in &eWarped Forests&r and &eThe End&r. They split upon contact, so you'll need to handle them with care!
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest27.title: Otherworldly Beings III
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest28.descrip: The Chocopedia mentions a breed that exists in the Nether that can fly...
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest28.title: Aerial Exploration
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest29.descrip: The &eAwful Ghast&r can be found in the &eAwful Dungeons&r of The Nether. Awful Dungeons can be found in &eSoul Sand Valleys and Warped Forests&r where they appear as floating circular structures. You can summon it by placing a &eNether Star in the altar&r.
&cNote that these structures cannot be found conventionally with the Explorer's Compass. You may need to find them manually.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest29.title: Defenders of the Nether
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest3.descript: Looks like your intuition was correct. The Nether Fortresses are crawling with weird enemies. The &eWither Skeleton&r seems to drop fragments of its skull, which seem to emanate some energy.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest3.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest3.title: Skull Collectors
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest30.descrip: Smithing templates are items used in &eSmithing Tables&r to alter tools and armor. They are consumed when used, but can be duplicated using an existing template, its material and diamonds.
Netherite upgrade smithing templates are required to upgrade diamond equipment with a &eNetherite Ingot&r. They are only found in &eBastion Remnants&r, and are guaranteed only in treasure rooms.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest30.title: Preparation
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest4.descript: It seems not all creatures here are innately hostile. But we need to show them we mean business.
It's time to take care of some &ePiglins&r and some &eHoglins&r. You'll find them most commonly in &eNether Wastes&r and &eCrimson Forests&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest4.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest4.title: Otherworldly Beings
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest5.descript: In The Nether, you may come across aggressive, fiery creatures. While the &eWyrm&r and &eBlaze&r seem powerful, it is almost certain that there are more powerful magma creatures given the size and depth of The Nether.
It might be best to be prepared against fire. Try finding and vanquishing these mobs to test yourself.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest5.subtitle: Fiery Adventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest5.title: Fiery Foes
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest6.descript: Surely you've already run into these flying critters... Something tells you that there are hundreds of these lurking around every corner.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest6.subtitle: Fiery Adventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest6.title: Flame Spitters
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest7.subtitle: Fiery Adventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest7.title: The Mother
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest8.descript: Place &eSoul Sand&r in a T shape, topped with &e3 Wither Skeleton Skulls&r to summon the &eWither.&r
This boss is very difficult, as it requires you to have a projectile weapon or spell to take care of its first phase. In the second phase, be prepared for the Wither to be up close and personal...
&o&eTip:&r &oHaving trouble defeating the Wither? Did you know that the higher level a mob is to you it takes less damage and deals more? This bonus starts at a 3 level differential.&r
&6This Quest Unlocks New Shops In the Campaign Shop.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest8.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest8.title: Dark Ritual
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest9.descript: Well, here is one of those big bads you've been tasked with removing. The &eWildfire&r is a powerful foe - you will need to be prepared.
The plates spinning around the &eWildfire&r need to be popped off before it can receive any damage.
You can find &eWildfire&r in &eCitadels.&r Alternatively, you can use a &eGateway of the Hellish Fortress&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest9.subtitle: Fiery Adventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.quest9.title: Ruler of Flames
ftbquests.chapter.act_ii.title: Act II
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest1.descrip: It seems like &eRotlings&r are not the only rotten creatures. There are many different breeds of them.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest1.title: Rotting Creatures
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest10.descri: This set of &earmor&r allows you to easily &emine and fight&r underwater. You can craft &eOxygen Tanks with Oxygelium&r found underwater in an &eIce Maze&r or from the &eTortured Souls&r also in that area. You can also consider using &eNeptunium&r gear from fishing to fight underwater!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest11.descri: These undead creatures have been spotted roaming the &eIce Mazes in the Shelter underground, near the Deep Frozen Ocean&r.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest11.title: The Restless Dead
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest12.descri: These fish definitely look fishy... and they're on land! These creatures can be found in &eIce Mazes near the Deep Frozen Ocean&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest12.title: Terrestrial Terrors
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest13.descri: The &eAnglerfish&r in the water of the &eIce Maze&r seem normal enough...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest13.title: Deep-Sea Creatures
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest14.descri: This slippery, slithery creature can be found in &eShelters&r by the &eIce Maze&r. Alternatively, you can find one in a &eGateway of the Ice Maze&r.
&cThe Eel spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest14.title: Slippery Swimmer
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest15.descri: Never actually seen by any living being, the myth of the &eMother of the Ice Maze&r is that it's a powerful creature that roams the seas, attacking sailors and pirates alike. If you want to find it, it's said that it has a chance of &espawning with Anglerfish&r (&75.0%% chance to spawn when an Anglerfish dies&r). It's also rumored that inside the gut of the creature, you'll find rare ancient pirate treasure...
&cThe Mother of the Ice Maze spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
The Mother of the Ice Maze Has:
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest15.title: Mother of the Deep Sea
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest16.descri: The &eGhost of Captain Cornelia&r appears when a certain sound is played from a &ehorn&r...
&6This Quest Unlocks New Shops In the Campaign Shop.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
Some say the &ehorn&r can be found in a &elarge mother-like creature&r that lurks in the &eDeep Frozen Ocean&r, or can be earned through the completion of a &eGateway of the Ice Maze&r.
The &eGhost of Captain Cornelia&r is resilient to death... She'll start &cregenerating her Health rapidly at once she's low&r. You can use a &eSplash Potion of Weakness&r to combat this.
&cThe Ghost of Captain Cornelia spawns at minimum level of 40.&r
The Ghost of Captain Cornelia Has:
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest16.title: Captain Cornelia's Locker
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest17.descri: These &ecold ingots&r can be used to craft some powerful &eFroststeel&r gear. They can be found in &eCold Underground Biomes&r such as the &eFrostfields, Frosty Smogstem Forest&r and &eIcy Sea&r biomes&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest17.title: Cold Ingots
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest18.descri: It's time for you to visit the Villager scholar. They might know something about alternate dimensions containing powerful creatures.
You can find the &eGatekeeper&r in &eGatekeeper Houses&r which can spawn in the &emountains, and plains or snowy villages&r.
You might need some way to coerce them to giving the information however... Perhaps some &aEmeralds&r?
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest18.title: The Gatekeepers
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest19.descri: Deep in the &eCatacombs&r, you may rarely find deactivated guardians. They say these guardians once protected the people from Rot creatures, but have long since lost their magic. Of course, that's just what they say...
If you cannot find these guardians, you may have better luck forcing a portal to the past with a &eGateway of the Labyrinth&r.
&cThe Forgotten Guardian spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest19.title: Hidden Secrets
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest2.descrip: You were told this dimension was dank and moist, but you did not expect it to be rotting. Kill the pesky &eRotlings&r that keep attacking you, they seem to be the cause of the rot.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest2.title: Foreign Enemies
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest20.descri: Having difficulty with some mobs? You can power yourself up using the Mine and Slash &eInfusing Profession&r. &eScrolls&r can be used on your Gear to &erandomly apply a beneficial stat&r. This stat can be upgraded by using subsequent Infusion Scrolls.
See the &eM\&S Professions&r chapter for more information on how to use this system!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest20.title: Not Vanilla Enchantments
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest21.descri: In CTE2, some Vanilla stats have compatibility with Mine and Slash!
Additionally, Enchantment compatibility is included. You can see how the Enchantment affects Mine and Slash stats in the &bEnchantment description in blue&r.

Enchantment stat bonuses cap out per item equal to the Max Enchantment level + 1. For example, Protection gives +2%% Armor, but each item with this enchantment can only give +12%%, even if you somehow get a Protection VIII item. However, there is also a global cap of the item cap x3. If you equipped 4 Protection VI items, you would receive only +36%% Armor.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest21.title: Stat and Enchantment Compatibility
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest22.descri: The &eImbuing Table&r allows you to &ecombine max level&r enchantments. You can use the table to raise a max level enchantment on an item by &e1&r. For example, using a &eFortune III&r book to upgrade a pickaxe that already has &eFortune III&r will result in a pickaxe with &eFortune IV&r.
&oNote that not all enchantments support this system.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest23.descri: &eAquamarine&r drop from &eDrowned, Squids, and Guardians&r and can be used to trade with &eSea Dwellers&r! Sea Dwellers are merfolk found in the &edepths of the ocean&r. You can usually find them congregated in &eunderwater villages&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest23.title: Sea Dwellers
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest24.descri: Resistances play an important role in keeping yourself alive. There are 3 Elements: &cFire&r, &bCold&r, and &eLightning&r. There are also &7Physical&r and &5Chaos&r damage types.

&6Elemental Damage&r becomes prominent from this point on, with most mobs having some form of Elemental Damage (whether innately, or from affixes). It is &evital&r that you maintain your &6Elemental Resistances&r! &5Chaos Resistance&r is also pivotal, albeit more elusive to obtain.
As you become stronger, the mobs also become stronger. &l&cEvery 25 levels, your resists permanently drop by 25%%!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest24.title: Resistances
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest25.descri: Try crafting your own Infusion Scroll at a higher rarity! Boot Infusions are particularly powerful as you can get special stats only found on Boot Infusions!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest25.title: Protecting the Feets
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest26.descri: The &eNaga&r are flying creatures found all around the &eUndergarden&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest26.title: Under the.. Undergarden?
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest27.descri: &eUmvuthi, the Sunbird&r is a large neutral boss mob - a heliomacer who serves as the creator of the Abavuthana.
Umvuthi can be found in a &eGateway of the Depths&r.
If approached by a player wearing a Umvuthana Mask, that player will be able to trade with Umvuthi.
&cThe Umvuthi spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest27.title: The Sun Chief
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest28.descri: &eFerrous Wroughtnauts&r start standing still, with their arms resting on their axe. When a player comes nearby or they are attacked, they ready themselves and start attacking. When not in combat Wroughtnauts will quickly regenerate their health.
&c&lWarning! Ferrous Wroughtnauts can break your shield. Proceed with caution!&r&r
You can find them in their Arenas throughout the Undergarden.
&cThe Ferrous Wroughtnaut spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest28.title: Steely Resolve
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest29.descri: Need a bit of an EXP boost? Consider eating &eExperience Gain Seafood&r crafted through Professions!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest29.title: Feeling A Bit Starved
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest3.descrip: Now that you're geared up, it's time to see what's causing these abnormalities in the ocean. You've noticed some activity in the &eOverworld Ice Mazes&r... It might be worth checking out. You can find these Ice Mazes on the &esurface near Deep Frozen Oceans&r.

They say a rare plant called &eOxygelium&r can be found at the &ebottom of these Ice Mazes&r in the ocean. Alternatively, you can find them from &eTortured Souls&r. If you're having trouble finding &eTortured Souls&r, you can try challenging the &eGateway of the Ice Maze&r!
You'll need &eBolt Armor&r to survive the cold. It can be crafted.
&cMobs from Aquamirae spawn at a minimum of level 35!&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest3.title: The Hidden Depths
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest30.descri: Ever wanted to go back to the Overworld to explore, but find the levels too low to make it an enjoyable experience? At certain breakpoint levels, past Dimensions will "enrage", increasing their level ranges for players to potentially go back and enjoy past Dimensions!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest30.title: Travelling Backwards
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest31.descri: &eTongbi, the Sculptor&r is a powerful geomancer mob that can be found in the &eMonastery&r.
Tongbi, the Sculptor can be found in a &eGateway of the Depths&r.
&cTongbi, the Sculptor spawns at minimum level of 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest31.title: The Sculptor
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest4.descrip: You spoke with a village local who claimed to once live in the Undergarden. According to that local, there were once powerful entities in temples throughout the land that protected civilization from creeping Rot. You'll need to find an Undergarden &eCatacombs&r to complete this quest.
However, it seems that was all in the past. New investigations hint at some new creatures haunting the Overworld depths.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest4.title: Civilization from the Past
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest5.descrip: You've heard from an elderly local from the village that they were originally from the Undergarden! According to the local, there are no longer any villages in the Undergarden due to the creeping Rot. However, there may still be resident &eStoneborn&r creatures willing to do trade.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest5.title: Foreign Friends
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest6.descrip: Explore the depths of the Undergarden and you'll find some useful resources that can be turned into gear!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest6.title: New Resources
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest7.descrip: If you're having trouble finding an &eEnd Portal Frame&r and running out of &eEye of Enders&r, perhaps it's time for you to craft an &eEndolocator&r, which is functionally the same as an Eye of Ender, but has unlimited uses!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest7.title: Structure-Finding Help
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest8.descrip: Complete all of the quests in Act III to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest8.title: Complete All Act III Quests!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.quest9.descrip: The &eStrange Fish Finder&r will locate nearby Devil's Hole chunks which contain &eunique fish and loot&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iii.title: Act III
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest1.descript: Your next task is to investigate &eThe End&r, and to slay the &eEnder Dragon&r. To get there, locate a &eStronghold&r, where you might find an &eEnd Portal Frame&r. You can activate the portal by slotting in &eEye of Enders&r into the frame.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest1.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest1.title: The End
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest10.descrip: You've defeated the Alchemist, and in their dying moments, they tell you about the Poison Dungeon.
&6This Quest Will Unlock New Shops In the Campaign Shop when the Quest:

The Poison Dungeon is a &elarge underground maze&r consisting of &e4 floors.&r Each floor will contain &e1 of 4 keys&r and a staircase leading down to the next floor. You will find a &elarge, hollowed maple tree covered in cobwebs on the surface&r.

Lurking in the lowest depths, you will find the &eArachnarch&r in her lair. This venomous foe lunges at targets and sprays poison from the ceiling above, but opportunities to stun the spider may arise if you're using a &oshield&r. According to the Gatekeeper, the Arachnarch becomes &estronger in the presence of more enemies&r (players).
Should you find a Poison Dungeon that's &ealready been cleared&r, you can go back to the &eGatekeeper&r and &epurchase the required keys&r.
You can also &eresummon&r the Arachnarch by using the &ePoisonous Arc&r obtained from it on a &ePoison Keystone&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest10.title: Caught in a Web
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest11.descrip: The Gatekeeper did mention there would be civilization in the Everbright and Everdawn. You'll just need to find it.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest11.title: Encampment
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest12.descrip: Apparently if you find a &eNightwatcher&r, they can provide you with a &eBag of Spoils&r which will let you &epickpocket villagers&r... Seems like a criminal activity that would be frowned upon by them...
That is, if they catch you.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest12.title: Pickpocketing
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest13.descrip: These dimensions seem to have plenty of natural resources. It might be worthwhile to harvest some to craft with.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest13.title: Mark My Words...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest14.descrip: According to the &9Blue Book&r, &5Falsite&r can be found in the &eEverbright&r and possesses a &estrengthening property&r. &6Horizonite&r can be found in the &eEverdawn&r and contains a &ehigh amount of heat&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest14.title: There's Trouble Brewin'
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest15.descrip: You've noticed sticks of different properties while exploring in the Everbright. Perhaps it's even possible to use these sticks to craft spears.
It might be a good idea to refer to the &9Blue Journal&r for the different types of sticks. I'm sure the Gatekeeper noticed this too.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest15.title: Spears and Sticks
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest16.descrip: You've noticed sticks of different properties while exploring in the Everdawn. Perhaps it's even possible to use these sticks to craft spears.
It might be a good idea to refer to the &9Blue Journal&r for the different types of sticks. I'm sure the Gatekeeper noticed this too.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest16.title: Sticks and Spears
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest17.descrip: The Everbright seems to be home to many foreign creatures. You can't really tell if they're mutations or natively weird.
Creatures here seem to be covered in ice...
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest17.title: Frozen in Time
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest18.descrip: The Everdawn seems to be home to many foreign creatures. You can't really tell if they're mutations or natively weird.
Here, they seem to be similar to insects you'd find in the Overworld.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest18.title: Creepy Crawlies
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest19.descrip: &eComplete&r all of the &equests&r in Act IV to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest19.title: Complete All Act IV Quests!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest2.descript: You've found the Gatekeeper... He's willing to give you information, but for a price.

You were able to learn from him (for free) that he's familiar with 2 alternate dimensions: the &eEverbright&r and the &eEverdawn&r. According to the Gatekeeper, it's possible to access these dimensions from the Overworld!

It also looks like he has some sort of &elighter for sale&r. Apparently it's needed to access the dimensions. Maybe it's fine to humour him and buy the lighter, but you'll need more information than that. Perhaps &esnooping around the house&r might prove fruitful...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest2.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest2.title: Wanderlust Ignition
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest20.descrip: In the Everbright and Everdawn dimensions, you will find &enew tameables and new potential mounts&r.
It might be fun to find a &eReindeer&r, &eCamel&r, or even a &eCosmic Fox&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest20.title: Mounts
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest21.descrip: The &eSnowcap Oven&r can utilize the special hot and cold properites of the &eSnowcap Mushroom as a fuel source&r.
Cooking with Snowcap Mushrooms will result in a &ecold byproduct&r that may be used elsewhere.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest21.title: The Wrong Kind
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest22.descrip: The &eHorizonite Forge&r can &esmelt things without any fuel&r due to the natural heat of the block. It does have a &erecharge time&r, but the forge can be recharged quicker using anything made of Horizonite or Sunstone.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest22.title: Really Hot Flames
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest23.descrip: The &eCodex&r is an accessory/gear item that you can equip, which starts dropping at level 50. The &eCodex&r provides powerful stats to the player depending on the diversity of gear equipped.
The bottom lists the requirements, such as: having 2 &cUniques&r, 1 piece of Normal gear (&7Common&r -> &5Mythic&r), and 2 &eRunewords&r equipped.
Depending on the number of these you have equipped, the &eCodex&r provides more stats which can be seen in the tooltip.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest23.title: Book of Records
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest24.descrip: If you're having trouble defending yourself from Elemental Damage, consider crafting a new Shield, Tome, or Totem!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest24.title: Defensive Traits
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest25.descrip: Increase your Core Attributes to gain an edge on your enemies...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest25.title: Alchemist's Brew
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest26.descrip: Find more creatures in the Everbright dimension for research!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest26.title: Frozen Predators
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest27.descrip: Find more creatures in the Everdawn dimension for research!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest27.title: Pestilence
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest28.descrip: The &eSupreme Bonescaller&r is a more powerful version of the &eBonescaller&r. It boasts increased defenses and has two combat phases. You can find them in &eDark Towers&r in the &eBlue Skies dimensions&r and &eThe End&r.
&cThe Surpeme Bonescaller spawns at minimum level 45 and Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest28.title: Unexpected Guests
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest29.descrip: The &eNightmare Stalker&r is an extremely dangerous spirit. The beginning of the Stalker's spawn is accompanied by a characteristic sound and a message in the chat. It also appears completely invisible - all you can see are two glowing eyes. While the Stalker is invisible, he moves much faster. When he receives damage, he loses invisibility.

The Nightmare Stalker notices the player from a great distance (up to 70 blocks), and immediately starts to stalk its victim. When attacking a victim, the stalker produces a piercing scream and causes blindness in its target, plunging them into complete darkness.
&cThe Nightmare Stalker spawns at minimum level 35.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest29.title: The Stalker
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest3.descript: Instead of relying on the portals that spawn in the &eGatekeeper's House&r, you can craft them yourself.
You can even buy the blocks from the &eTrading Terminal/Barter Board&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest3.subtitle: Deterministic Portal
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest3.title: Deterministic Portals
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest30.descrip: The &eLifestealer&r is a fearsome undead creature. After receiving enough damage, he reveals his true form by screaming. The &eLifestealer&r also periodically begins to cause a dark splash that pushes the player and all nearby creatures to the side, causing them damage.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest30.title: Vitality Drainer
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest31.descrip: Need a bit of an EXP boost? Consider eating &eExperience Gain Seafood&r crafted through Professions!
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest31.title: Everbright Aquarium
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest4.descript: BRRrrr, it is really cold in this dimension... But you made it! The &eEverbright&r is a dimension in perpetual frost. Now would be a good time to refer to the &9Blue Journal&r...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest4.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest4.title: The Everbright
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest5.descript: Yowza, it is really warm and humid in this dimension... But you made it! The &eEverdawn&r is a dimension filled with creature mutations and high heat. Now would be a good time to refer to the &9Blue Journal&r...
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest5.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest5.title: The Everdawn
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest6.descript: The Gatekeeper is also keen on giving away his &ejournal&r. It looks really old and musty, but according to the keeper, it contains a lot of &einformation&r on the dimensions.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest6.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest6.title: Your Personal Blue Skies Guide
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest7.descript: To access the Everbright's &eNature Dungeon,&r you will first need to find the dimension's &eBlinding Dungeon&r... According to the Gatekeeper, you'll be able to &efind the Summoner&r in the Everbright's Blinding Dungeon.

Be careful with the Summoner, as the Summoner fights with lightning and energy attacks, and has golems to guard themselves! According to the Gatekeeper, the Summoner becomes &estronger in the presence of more enemies&r (players).
Defeat the Summoner, and you will be able to access the Nature Dungeon!
You can also &eresummon&r the Summoner, by using the &eEthereal Arc&r obtained from it on a &eBlinding Keystone (Everbright)&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest7.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest7.title: A New Headquarters
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest8.descript: To access the Everdawn's &ePoison Dungeon,&r you will first need to find the dimension's &eBlinding Dungeon&r... According to the Gatekeeper, you'll be able to &efind the Alchemist&r in the Everdawn's Blinding Dungeon.

Watch out for potions and physical constructs as you fight the Alchemist. A lot of the Alchemist's attacks are telegraphed, but missing these cues may cost you an arm! According to the Gatekeeper, the Alchemist becomes &estronger in the presence of more enemies&r (players).
Defeat the Alchemist, and you will be able to access the Poison Dungeon!
You can also &eresummon&r the Alchemist by using the &eDusk Arc&r obtained from it on a &eBlinding Keystone (Everdawn)&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest8.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest8.title: A New Laboratory
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest9.descript: You've defeated the Summoner, and in their dying moments, they tell you about the Nature Dungeon.
&6This Quest Will Unlock New Shops In the Campaign Shop when the Quest:
The Nature Dungeon is a &emassive green labyrinth&r consisting of &e4 floors.&r Each floor will contain &e1 of 4 keys&r and a staircase leading up to the next floor.

Resting at the top of the floor you will find the &eStarlit Crusher&r. This slow-moving tree packs a punch, but opportunities to stun the tree may arise if you're using a &ospear&r. According to the Gatekeeper, the Starlit Crusher becomes &estronger in the presence of more enemies&r (players).
Should you find a Nature Dungeon that's &ealready been cleared&r, you can go back to the &eGatekeeper&r and &epurchase the required keys&r.
You can also &eresummon&r the Starlit Crusher by using the &eNature Arc&r obtained from it on a &eNature Keystone&r.
&cThe Starlit Crusher spawns at minimum level 50.&r
