ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.quest9.title: An Uprooting
ftbquests.chapter.act_iv.title: Act IV
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest1.descripti: This is it! &eKill the Ender Dragon&r and put an "end" to this madness!
&oIt looks like you might be able to do something with the wing...&r
&cThe Ender Dragon spawns at minimum level 50.&r
&b+25%% Physical Damage converted to Lightning Damage&r
&b+25%% Physical Damage converted to Chaos Damage&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest1.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest1.title: Dragon Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest10.descript: Although you've &eslain the dragon&r, you will still find remnants of the beast. Slay its &espawn&r to prevent any further devastation.
Note that you can &erespawn&r the Ender Dragon again using &eEnd Crystals&r! The method is the same as &eVanilla.&r
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest10.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest10.title: Dragonspawn
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest11.descript: Rumours have it that &eFarseer&r are prophetic creatures originating from the void.
&o&7Completion unlocks a Repeatable Quest in the Repeatables Chapter!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest11.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest11.title: They Knew You Were Coming
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest12.descript: &eComplete&r all of the &equests&r in Act V to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest12.subtitle: Achievement
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest12.title: Complete All Act V Quests!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest13.descript: Maps are &einstanced dungeons&r from Mine and Slash. To get one, you will need to find a Map or &ecraft a Map Creator&r, which will generate a &7Common&r &eMap&r. These can only be used &estarting at level 55&r.
Maps can also roll various affixes. &eAffixes&r are random modifiers that are applied either to the players (usually negative) or the mobs (usually positive).

To access these instanced dungeons, you will also need to craft a &eMap Device&r. The Maps can be used on the Map Device block (once placed). You also will need to craft a &ePearl of Home&r, to ensure you can return safely.
Maps will have an associated Rarity and Tier. The Rarity and Tier determine the difficulty of mobs, and quality of loot.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest13.subtitle: Level Methods
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest13.title: Maps
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest14.descript: Now that you have the necessary materials, try taking on a &eMap!&r

As you're killing mobs and exploring the Map, you will notice your kill percent increasing. This kill percent can be considered as an exploration metric. The higher your exploration, the better the rewards will be for killing the &eMap Boss&r. As you explore more, your exploration rarity will start to go up, starting with &auncommon&r exploration as the first milestone.

You will need to find the teleporter block to warp you to the &cBoss Room&r. This block can spawn anywhere within the map. Kill the &cBoss&r to gain access to a specialized &cReward Room&r, &dwhere the loot improves depending on your progress percent&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest14.subtitle: Level Methods
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest14.title: Dimensional Conundrum
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest15.descript: &eElytras&r can now be customized with &ebanners, dyes, capes, armor trims, and glow squid ink&r.
&eCombine&r a full elytra with any banners, dyes, armor trims, and/or glow ink squid to &eapply&r it to the whole elytra.
Place a &efull elytra&r into a crafting grid to seperate it into &etwo elytra wings&r which allows you to customize them seperately and then craft them back &etogether.&r
You can also hide your &ecape&r from appearing on your elytra by just crafting it with a piece of &epaper.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest15.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest15.title: Customizable Elytras
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest16.descript: &eEnderiophages&r can be found where &eChorus Fruit grows&r. These things spread &edisease&r and should be taken care of as soon as possible! Enderiophages can be built by placing an &eEnd Rod with a Capsid on top and placing an Eye of Ender inside&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest16.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest16.title: Viral Disaster
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest17.descript: &eShriek Worms&r are terrifying creatures found on &eThe Otherside&r. Should you &estep on Infested Sculk&r, you'll be met with a surprise.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest17.subtitle: Deeper and Darker
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest17.title: Death Traps
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest18.descript: &eStalkers&r are creepy and powerful entities that can be found on &eThe Otherside&r. They're attracted to harsh noises, so if you're looking to find one, maybe &ebreak some jars&r?
If you're not having any luck, consider the &eGateway of Darkness&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest18.subtitle: Deeper and Darker
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest18.title: Uncanny Presence
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest19.descript: In &eEchoing Forests&r, you'll find creatures that have lost their souls. Once thought to be human, now a husk of its former self.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest19.subtitle: Deeper and Darker
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest19.title: The Hopeless
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest2.descripti: You received &einstructions&r from a village guard, scribbled on a loose piece of bamboo.
&o2. Obtain End Crystals from the island.&r
The rest has been &erubbed off &rfrom your adventures.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest2.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest2.title: Summoning Ritual
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest20.descript: Catch the Ender Flu effect. Cure it by eating many &eChorus Fruit&r or &edrinking Milk&r. The clock is ticking...
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest20.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest20.title: A Race Against the Clock
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest21.descript: Within Mine and Slash Maps, there are additional events that can occur.
&eAncient Obelisk&r: chests have a chance to spawn special wave-based Maps that can be used on &eObelisks&r!

&eProphecy Event&r: find altars in Maps that can apply curses to yourself. The more curses you have on yourself, the more &eProphecy Coins&r you can earn from killing mobs. Use these Coins to purchase rewards in Maps via the Hub! Note that &6Legendary&r and &5Mythic&r rewards only appear starting in Tier 20 and 40 maps, respectively.
&eHarvest Event&r: find portals in Maps that will bring you to a new Harvest room. Kill mobs in these rooms to earn &eChaotic Gems&r, which are used to craft special currencies!
&eRelics&r: find Relics from killing Bosses that can be slotted into the &eMap Device&r to juice up your Maps! Interact with the Map Device using a Relic Key to slot Relics in!

Additionally, &cBosses&r in &6Legendary&r and &5Mythic&r Maps have a chance to drop &eUber Fragments&r. When combined, they turn into &eDungeon Map Uber Upgrades&r. You can use these on Maps to face &eUber Bosses&r - very powerful creatures that drop lots of loot. They can even drop special &eUnique Jewels&r!
The pool of Uber Bosses consist of old bosses you faced during the campaign, and some new ones!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest21.subtitle: Lost Page
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest21.title: More Map Content
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest22.descript: Despite it's expensive ingredients, the &eDisenchanter&r allows you to take all the enchantments off of an item for a &edurability cost&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest22.subtitle: Utilities
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest23.descript: Two new structures can be found in the End: &eEnd Tower and Catacombs&r. You can find fun new loot in these structures, as well as &ePurpur Golems&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest23.subtitle: Outer End
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest23.title: Structures Beyond Reach
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest24.descript: You'll find &eStalkers, Entombed&r, and many other vicious creatures of the void in new End biomes. Check out the &eAzure Forest, Crystal Crag, and Primordial Pools&r!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest24.subtitle: Outer End
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest24.title: Residents of the Void
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest25.descript: The Shelterer is an interesting creature that can be found in the &eEnd Dungeon&r. By releasing it with a &eNether Star&r on its &eAltar&r, it will be able to lock itself in an energetic shulker box.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest25.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest25.title: Boxed In
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest26.descript: After slaying &ePhantom Creepers&r in the &eNether&r, you've found yourself face-to-face with them again in &eThe End&r!
&ePhantom Creepers&r spawn in &eWarped Forests&r and &eThe End&r. They split upon contact, so you'll need to handle them with care!
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest26.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest26.title: Otherworldly Beings IV
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest27.descript: You feel like you've seen an indentation that might fit the &eVoid Worm Eye&r perfectly... Where was it again?.. In the &ebadlands&r back home? Some &eAncient Ruins&r? Maybe I can call on it with a &eGateway of the Remnant&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest27.subtitle: Home Sweet Home
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest27.title: Samurai Warrior
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest3.descripti: You've slain the dragon, but it seems like there's more to The End than you thought. It might be a good time to use your newly found &eElytra&r and &eexplore the outer islands&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest3.subtitle: Endventure
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest3.title: Beyond
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest4.descripti: You learned that it wasn't the Ender Dragon that was causing the rifts to open. As vile as the beast was, it was just that - a vile beast.
It seems there is more to The End than you thought... Some say another danger lurks in the void, and others say throwing a &eMysterious Worm&r into it will draw it out.
You can obtain a &eMysterious Worm&r by killing the &eEnder Dragon&r.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest4.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest4.title: Genetic Modification
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest5.descripti: Be prepared, as this horrific creature has many parts to its body. Additionally, it will fire homing crystals at nearby players. If you get too close you'll get knocked back. Be careful not to get knocked off the island!
&cThe Void Worm spawns at minimum level 55.&r
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest5.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest5.title: Worm from the Void
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest6.descripti: There's a report of an underground creature wrecking havoc underground traders. Some call it a &e"Warden".&r Some say it can be found in the &eAncient City&r deep underneath.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest6.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest6.title: Back Home
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest7.descripti: The &eWarden&r is completely blind, and relies on vibrations, smell, and touch to detect players. You can evade them by sneaking, or using diversions such as arrows. It has a powerful melee attack, and can release a devastating homing sonic boom attack.
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest7.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest7.title: Dimension Guardian
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest8.descripti: Wow, who'd have thought? The &eWarden&r seemed to be protecting a gateway to a new &eundiscovered dimension&r. What's on &eThe Otherside&r?
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest8.subtitle: Main Quest
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest8.title: The Otherside
ftbquests.chapter.act_v.quest9.descripti: Continue to &eexplore,&r and descend into the &edarkness...&r You never know what &egoodies&r you'll find.

&b&oCongratulations on completing the main Campaign! Continue onto the Epilogue for completion or move on to explore what the worlds have to offer. Be sure to check out the Side Quests! Keep on leveling and customizing your character, and try to overcome the greatest challenges in Maps!&r&r

ftbquests.chapter.completionist.quest1.d: &l&eWow. You've completed all side quests! Congratulations!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.completionist.title: Completionist
ftbquests.chapter.culinary_delights.ques: &eFarmer's Delight&r overhauls the process of cooking. You can now create a &estove&r to heat a &epot or skillet&r that is placed ontop of it.
&ePots&r are able to make stews and combined foods that are held with bowls. You can also make Feasts which are foods that are placed, like cakes, that have multiple portions.
&eSkillets&r can cook food over campfires or stoves.

&eCutting Boards &rare useful in and out of cooking. Not only can you &efillet fish and meats&r into strips, but also cut up various items such as wool into string or leather armor into leather. Cutting boards require &eknives&r primarily but some recipes will use pickaxes, axes, shears, etc.
Slay an &eElder Guardian&r for its meat.
&eFarmer's Delight&r also provides three basic resources that can be used to make decorations, such as hanging signs, to utilities, such as climable ropes and safety nets.
&eTree barks&r are obtained from using an axe on wood placed on a cutting board and can be used to make paper.
&eCanvas&r is made from straw and can replace leather in all recipes as well as make rugs and other decorations.
&eStraw&r is obtained from cutting grass with a knife and can be used in numerous recipes such as crafting leads.
&eClams&r can be caught and opened on a &ecutting board&r which can sometimes result in precious &epearls.&r Even if they're useless, they're still shiny.
Use a &eknife&r on &ePropelplants&r to collect them. They can be planted on &eNetherrack, Nylium, or Rich Soul Soil&r.
The &eMachete&r is a better weapon to use for killing/harvesting &eHoglins&r over the knife. It may be a worthy weapon by itself.
&eCrabber's Delight &rallows you to catch crabs and other treasures using &eCrab Pots&r. They require bait, usually fish, in them to catch anything. They can also be fished up with a fishing rod.

&eCooking for Blockheads&r makes your cooking experience a lot easier. Aside from Farmer's Delight which requires you to use the Pot, with Cooking for Blockhead you can cook all the food in one place. No recipe juggling necessary.
The &eStove&r can cook multiple foods at once and can take FE as energy if upgraded.
The &eSink&r provides infinite water for buckets and recipes.
The &eCooking Table&r is able to pull food and tools from blocks and fridges connected by Kitchen Floors.
There are more pieces to add to your kitchen as well such as the &etoaster&r or &espice rack&r.
These blocks allow &eRefined Storage&r and &eCooking for Blockheads&r to work together!
The &eKitchen Station&r needs to be placed as part of the kitchen network and the &eKitchen Access Point&r needs to be placed in the Refined Storage Network.

The Kitchen Station acts as &eRefined Storage Network Receiver&r and needs to be attached to a &eKitchen Network Card&r. Then this Kitchen Network Card needs to be &eplaced inside&r a &eKitchen Access Point&r, which acts as a Refined Storage Network Transmitter.
Once everything is connected, you'll be able to &econsume items&r from your Refined Storage network right from your cooking table.
&eUndergarden Delight&r adds new &eknives and some food recipes&r to Undergarden food items!
You can fillet &eGwibling&r meat and cook it to get &eCooked Gwiblin Fillets&r!
&eShimmerpearls&r can be harvested from &eTall Shimmerweed&r on a &eCutting Board&r.
&eGloomgourd Pie&r can be made from various &eUndergarden&r ingredients!
&eBrewin' And Chewin'&r allows you to brew and ferment new foods.
As soon as you get a &eKeg&r, you may enter the world of fermenting. Here you may create drinks, cheese, and light snacks.

You can put &eup to 4 ingredients&r inside a keg, with an additional slot for fluids such as water, honey, and milk, which can be filled through the container slot. You will also want to keep the temperature of the keg in mind, which may change depending on the biome you're in or blocks surrounding it. If the ingredients, fluid and temperature match, the keg will start brewing.

Most drinks require a &eTankard&r to be served in, and cheeses require &eHoneycomb&r to be served. The finished fluid slot will tell you which item to use. Simply add the container to the corresponding slot or right-click the pot with it to take a serving!
You can place heating or cooling blocks near the keg to turn up or down the heat!
Use the keg to create Mead and Cheese!
While holding a &ehoe,&r right click crops to harvest without breaking them. Higher tiered &ehoes&r harvest in bigger radiuses.
Looking to wind down? Farmer's Respite adds &eteas and other beverages&r you can drink! Farmer's Respite also has compatibility with Brewin' And Chewin' &eKegs&r!
Using a pot and specific ingredients, you can brew your own teas!
&eFarming for Blockheads&r makes farming easier by providing &ethree&r different fertilizers to apply to farmland. Only one fertilizer can be used on a farmland.
The &eMarket&r block allows you to buy seeds and sapling directly.
The &eFeeding Trough&r lets animals breed automatically if supplied with food.
The &eChicken Nest&r automatically collects any eggs laid near it.
&eNether's Delight&r adds food and utility to the Nether such as &eHoglin Loins&r and&e Strider meats&r.
Obtain 3 Hoglin Loins and 3 Strider Slices
&eCorn Delight&r adds corn products such as tortillas, nachos, popcorn, and more.
&eCuisine Delight&r allows you to cook &eany compatible food&r to create new, unique foods.

While near a fire source, hold the &eCuisine Skillet&r in your &eoffhand.&r You can also place it on the fire source. &eRight click food&r in hand to add it to the &eskillet.&r Two gauges will appear on the right side of your screen for each ingredient added. The &efirst gauge&r is how cooked the food is, you want it to end in the &eyellow.&r The &esecond gauge&r is how &eburned each side&r is, you want the pointer inside to &eremain within the green&r. Then, use the &espatula&r to &eflip&r all the ingredients inside to keep either side from &eburning.&r Use the &ebowl&r quickly to take the food out.
Hold &eshift&r on food in inventory to see cooking timings.
If it's &eburned,&r you let the second gauge get into the red on one of the sides.
If it's &enot cooked enough,&r you didn't let the first gauge get into the yellow.
&eEnder's Delight &radds food and ingredient drops to each mob in the End. These can be cooked into foods that can assist you navigating the End.
&eOcean's Delight &radds food drops to numerous creatures within the oceans
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest1.descri: At long last... your notes from your various adventures have brought you to &eThe Otherside&r.

From your humble beginnings in the &eOverworld&r, perilous journey through the &eNether&r and &eUnderdark&r, encounters in the &eEverbright and Everdawn&r, and close calls in &eThe End&r, you are now at what you believe to be the source of the evil.

What did that researcher back at the village call it? A "quarrel" amongst the &bPrimal Titans&r? According to the researcher, it should be enough to just eliminate the four elemental creatures found in &eThe End&r and &eThe Otherside&r.
Get ready to embark on your final quest! There's no way they'll come back after killing them, right..?
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest1.title: Primal Evils
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest10.descr: &eHedge Spiders&r are almost identical to normal overworld Spiders aside from their distinct green color. The only difference between this species and their Overworld counterparts is that they are able to spawn in brightly-lit &eHedge Mazes&r. Legend says these Spiders are brave enough to protect their treasure from explorers.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest10.title: Trapped
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest11.descr: Rising out of the otherwise flat terrain of the &eTwilight Forest&r, &eHollow Hills&r are conspicuous mounds of dirt that conceal a vast hollow cave packed with stalactites and stalagmites, some of which are formed with precious ores and minerals. Explorers have found these large dome-shaped hills in three distinct sizes, large enough to be detected and marked on a &eMagic Map&r, with an appropriate icon indicating its size. Some hills have been found to have other caves and ravines cutting into them, so those adventurers tunneling beneath the earth may find themselves inside one of these hills in their explorations.

Small hills measure approximately 30 metres across and usually contain the basic minerals such as Coal, Iron, with Redstone and Glowstone occasionally being found. Explorers can expect to find a couple of treasure chests containing common tools and items such as food, arrows, ladders and torches, but rarely, a valuable Ore Magnet may be found along with a Diamond and &eSteeleaf&r. &eSwarm Spiders, Redcap Goblins and Kobolds&r reside in these hills, with other creatures common to the Overworld as well.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest11.title: A Small Mound
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest12.descr: The &eNaga Courtyard&r is a large, walled-off structure built from various forms of stone and Nagastone. Inside the Naga Courtyard, there are various hedges of Twilight Oak Leaves, pillars of Polished Andesite and Etched Nagastone, and additional decorative features. In the middle of the structure is a Boss Spawner containing the &eNaga&r.
Slaying the Naga will unlock the next structure in Progression, the &eLich Tower&r, removing the structure's protection.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest12.title: Solely Snakular
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest13.descr: The &eLich Tower&r consists of a single central spire, from which many smaller towers and chambers jut out haphazardly from the sides, connecting directly into the next tower or via bridge.

Numerous hostile mobs spawn in the Lich Tower normally or through spawners. The usual assortment of vanilla mobs naturally spawn. The &eDeath Tome&r also spawns in the Lich Tower, and is the only structure to find this mob.
In order to unlock the Lich Tower, the &eNaga&r must be defeated.
The Lich Tower is also key to unlocking three other biomes and structures:
- Dispelling the mosquito swarm around the &eTwilight Swamp&r, allowing exploration into the &eLabyrinth&r.
- Dispelling the darkness covering the &eDark Forest&r, allowing exploration into the &eGoblin Knight Stronghold&r.
- Dispelling the blizzard over the &eSnowy Forest&r, allowing entry into the &eYeti Cave&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest13.title: The Tower of Power
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest14.descr: &eDeath Tomes&r are dangerous books float in the staircases and libraries of the Lich Tower. They fire a projectile that slows the creature they hit for a brief time. Death Tomes occasionally lose pages when struck.
Death Tomes may also drop a &eMagic Map Focus&r upon death.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest14.title: Enchanted Library
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest15.descr: The &eTwilight Lich&r, also referred to as just the &eLich&r, resides on the top of a &eLich Tower&r. It appears as a tall humanoid skeleton wearing a purple cape, and a large golden crown. Surrounding itself is a wall of shields to protect itself from any damage, but maybe they can be broken.
&6This Quest Unlocks New Shops In the Campaign Shop.
Adventurers report that the projectiles can be deflected with a properly-timed swing. Every time the player reflects the Lich's projectiles back at it, it will remove one of its shields.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest15.title: The Master Mastermind
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest16.descr: &eHollow Hills&r are an excellent source of ores to be mined and treasures to be looted, with the more valuable loot contained in the larger hills. The treasure to be found tend to be guarded by the dangerous creatures abound in the darkness, which can easily spell the doom of an unwary explorer. Unfortunately, it also seems that the larger the hill, the more creatures that live there. Explorers should remember the basic rules of caving, and never tunnel directly down into a Hollow Hill; instead, approach from the side and bring plenty of light sources to illuminate the interior as various spawners are around every corner.

Medium hills are about 60 metres in diameter, and 25+ metres tall in the center. The interior becomes more spacious, but this has also attracted more and deadlier creatures. Gold and Lapis can be found here as well as Diamond and Emerald being seen, albeit rarely. As the value of the ores increase, so do the treasures - up to 6 chests can usually be found in a medium hill containing &eIronwood Ingots and Transformation Powder&r, along with unique artifacts such as a Moonworm Queen, and the Peacock Feather Fan. &eFire Beetles and Redcap Sappers&r live in medium hills, in addition to those seen in small hills.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest16.title: A Medium Mound
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest17.descr: The &eLabyrinth&r is a major landmark tied to Progression. It appears as an unremarkable small hill in the &eTwilight Swamp&r, but opens up to an entrance to an underground structure of sprawling maze passages. In the deep levels of the Labyrinth, a fenced-off room contains a Boss Spawner with the &eMinoshroom&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest17.title: A Magic Maze
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest18.descr: The &eMinoshroom&r is a mini-boss found lurking in its prison at the bottom of a &eLabyrinth&r. The &eMinoshroom&r is a bizarre hybrid of a Mooshroom and a Minotaur.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest18.title: Mooooooooo
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest19.descr: &eMaze Slimes&r are &eTwilight Forest&r variants of the Overworld Slime. These Slimes have a gray color and spawn in &eLabyrinths&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest19.title: Concrete... Slime?
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest2.descri: The &eNight Lich&r resides in the &eLich Tower&r found in &eCrystal Crags&r in &eThe End&r. To find the structure, you can craft &eSoul Stars&r. Using the &eSoul Star&r will consume it to indicate the direction of the nearest &eLich Tower&r!
&cThe Night Lich spawns at minimum level of 90 and at Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest2.title: The Frozen Tower
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest20.descr: Resembling a large &eHollow Hill&r, the &eHydra Lair&r looms out of the flat swamplands providing a natural lair for the multi-headed Hydra at the heart of the &eFire Swamp&r. The &eHydra&r seems to have hollowed out the hill itself, providing it with shelter and forcing anyone who would challenge it to approach it head on. The area that faces the Hydra is also bereft of vegetation or cover, so that there is nowhere to hide.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest20.title: The Lair
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest21.descr: The &eHydra&r can be found in a unique, cracked-open &eHydra Lair&r in a &eFire Swamp&r. The Hydra appears as a massive creature, with two stumpy legs supporting a wide, scaly body. Multiple serpentine necks end in heads that bear a passing resemblance to that of the Ender Dragon. The entire creature is covered in blue-green and dark blue scales.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest21.title: Multi-Headed Beast
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest22.descr: &eMeef Stroganoff&r can be quite filling and potent, allowing any adventurer the energy to explore even the hottest of biomes after eating a single bowl.
Upon consuming &eMeef Stroganoff&r, the player is granted access to both the &eFire Swamp&r and the &eHydra&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest22.title: A Hearty Meal
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest23.descr: The &eDark Forest&r is a densely vegetated biome with a thick canopy that blocks almost all light from the forest floor. Darkwood Trees grow most commonly in this biome, with some smaller vegetation such as tall grass and flowers covering the grass. Occasionally, Mushglooms grow in the area, providing a faint source of light.

Despite the darkness, monsters such as Zombies and Skeletons will not spawn on the surface. However, other monsters such as &eMist Wolves&r and &eKing Spiders&r will spawn, making traversal in the Dark Forest more dangerous.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest23.title: Blinded By Darkness
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest24.descr: &eMist Wolves&r are mysterious monsters that spawn in the &eDark Forest&r. These monsters wait for their victim in the darkness of the forest. They will turn transparent in the dark, and only take solid form in areas of bright light. If they attack a player in the dark, their attacks can blind the player, but this affliction doesn't seem to happen near a light source.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest24.title: Hidden In The Mist
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest25.descr: &eKing Spiders&r can only be found in the &eDark Forest&r and the first sign of them is usually their red eyes glowing in the darkness. They are twice the size, speed and toughness of normal spiders, and are found with a &eSkeleton Druid&r riding on their back, making them a formidable foe to deal with.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest25.title: Fear-Inducing Spiders
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest26.descr: Ancient and forlorn, the &eGoblin Knight Strongholds&r rest amongst the unnaturally dense canopy of the &eDark Forest&r. They can be difficult to spot amongst the darkness with little remaining above ground apart from broken walls and cracked bricks. Beneath the surface however, is a vast complex of rooms and hallways and tombs.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest26.title: Goblin Usurpers
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest27.descr: The &eKnight Phantoms&r reside in a brick-layered room in the &eGoblin Knight Stronghold&r. They appear as ghostly pieces of Phantom Armor, and appear to have a spooky, ghost-like look when attacking.
Defeating the &e6 Knight Phantoms&r will progress the Dark Forest Phase, thus removing the protections over the &eDark Forest Center&r, now allowing access to the &eUr-Ghast&r and its &eDark Tower&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest27.title: Cursed Knights
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest28.descr: Rising ominously over the &eDark Forest&r are the awe-inspiring &eDark Towers&r. So tall that they seem to pierce the sky itself, these immense constructions contain all manner of unique rooms, obstacles, treasure and monsters to explore and fight on the epic journey to the top. They are generally found at the very heart of a Dark Forest, where the thick, choking foliage becomes a strange, autumn-like hue, perhaps due to the sinister influence of the Tower.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest28.title: Dark Spires
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest29.descr: The &eUr-Ghast&r can be found flying around at the top of the gigantic &eDark Tower&r. It appears as a large, supernatural creature with many large, waving tentacles. It is covered in what adventurers fear is blood, or could be Carminite. This creature resembles the Carminite Ghastguard, due to their ability to stalk players, as well as the Nether-exclusive Ghasts, but is much larger and much more dangerous.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest29.title: Blood, or Carminite?
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest3.descri: The &eNether Gauntlet&r can be found in &eGauntlet Arenas&r located in the &eBlooming Caverns&r biome of &eThe Otherside&r.
&cThe Nether Gauntlet spawns at minimum level of 90 and at Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest3.title: The Ardent Depths
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest30.descr: The &eSnowy Forest&r is an unnaturally cold biome that stands in stark contrast to much of the rest of the Twilight Forest dimension. Explorers have noted many similarities to the Overworld Snowy Taiga biome, with snow covering the ground between large stands of spruce trees growing in place of the usual Canopy Trees, with some growing just as large. Why such an icy climate should exist in a predominantly mild and wet dimension is a mystery, though some suspect the large &eGlacier&r that nestles amongst the fir trees may have something to do with it.

Care must be taken when trekking through these areas, as &eWinter Wolves&r roam the land along with dangerous &eYetis&r. These creatures should be approached cautiously, especially when delving into the &eYeti Caves&r, as many dozens can be found in these icy caves. The &eAlpha Yeti&r who claim dominion over their kin will not take kindly to intrusion from the curious, or the foolish.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest30.title: Snowed In
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest31.descr: &eWinter Wolves&r are large, bluish-white canines that live in the &eSnowy Forest&r. These monsters resembles their &eDark Forest&r counterparts, but with a white coat. They pursue and attack players in their territory, attacking with both their bite and with a freezing breath attack that applies a slow.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest31.title: A Chilling Awooo
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest32.descr: &eYeti&r are large, white, blocky creatures that live in the &eSnowy Forest&r or within the &eYeti Cave&r. They are semi-passive in nature, only taking interest in players that move within about 4 blocks of them. They have no directly damaging attacks. Instead, they will pick up their target and throw them in a random direction. Adventurers will not be directly harmed by a Yeti, but the fall damage will hurt. If attacked, they will become aggressive, pursuing their target.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest32.title: The Yeti
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest33.descr: The fearsome &eAlpha Yeti&r is much larger than the rest of the Yetis in its pack. It has six large blue horns and is constantly aggressive towards players.
Defeating the &eAlpha Yeti&r will remove the protections over the &eGlacier&r, now allowing access to the &eSnow Queen&r and its &eAurora Palace&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest33.title: The Alpha Yeti
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest34.descr: Standing over the desolate &eGlaciers&r, the &eAurora Palace&r is a striking and beautiful sight for adventurers in the Twilight Forest, with its walls constantly shifting in a variety of colours reaching into the sky. Beauty is only skin deep sadly, as there are dangers within who will not hesitate to defend their magical home.

Adventurers who defeat the &eAlpha Yeti&r will find that just &etouching his downy fur&r will transfer a portion of the magical power from the fur to themselves, dispelling the winds and leaving the Palace open for exploration.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest34.title: A Palace of Beauty
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest35.descr: The &eSnow Queen&r is found in an ice-encrusted room near the top of the &eAurora Palace&r. She is perhaps the most human-like creature encountered in the Twilight Forest, resembling a woman, but with large violet eyes and pale blue skin. She is immediately hostile towards any intruders in her vicinity, launching quickly into one of three main attack patterns.

The Snow Queen flies on a saucer-shaped sled of magical ice blocks. The sled serves a powerful defense, both moving the Queen up and out of range of close-range attacks, while &eblocking any ranged attacks made from below&r. To successfully attack the Snow Queen, you must anticipate her movements, &eattacking only when she moves close enough that you can get around the ice sled&r.
&cThe Snow Queen spawns at Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest35.title: The Queen of Ice
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest36.descr: Upon killing the &eNaga&r, a &eRespawn Monument&r will appear. You can activate the block to respawn the boss by placing &eBlocks of Ironwood&r in a circle around the monument like so:
Then, on the monument itself, place a &eWither Skeleton Skull&r facing &eNorth&r. &cIt MUST face North!&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest37.descr: Upon killing the &eLich&r, a &eRespawn Monument&r will appear. You can activate the block to respawn the boss by placing &eBlocks of Gold&r in a circle around the monument like so:
Then, on the monument itself, place a &eWither Skeleton Skull&r facing &eNorth&r. &cIt MUST face North!&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest38.descr: Upon killing the &eUr-Ghast&r, a &eRespawn Monument&r will appear. You can activate the block to respawn the boss by placing &eCrying Obsidian&r in a circle around the monument like so:
Then, on the monument itself, place a &eWither Skeleton Skull&r facing &eNorth&r. &cIt MUST face North!&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest39.descr: Upon killing the &eSnow Queen&r, a &eRespawn Monument&r will appear. You can activate the block to respawn the boss by placing &eBlocks of Lapis Lazuli&r in a circle around the monument like so:
Then, on the monument itself, place a &eWither Skeleton Skull&r facing &eNorth&r. &cIt MUST face North!&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest4.descri: You can find the &eVoid Blossom&r in the &eEchoing Forests&r of &eThe Otherside&r. You can use &eVoid Lilies&r to indicate the direction to these &eVoid Blossoms&r.
&cThe Void Blossom spawns at minimum level of 90 and at Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest4.title: The Sprawling Vinelands
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest40.descr: Upon killing the &eHydra&r, a &eRespawn Monument&r will appear. You can activate the block to respawn the boss by placing &eAncient Debris&r in a circle around the monument like so:
Then, on the monument itself, place a &eWither Skeleton Skull&r facing &eNorth&r. &cIt MUST face North!&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest41.descr: Defeat all of the &eTwilight Forest&r bosses in the Epilogue to complete this achievement. Then show it off with your new hat!
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest41.title: Defeat All Twilight Forest Bosses!
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest42.descr: Nestled among the hills and fir trees of the &eSnowy Forest&r, the &eYeti Cave&r appears as a square-shaped hill. Four entrances allow access to the ice-covered insides, where packed ice hangs from stalactites and covers the floor. Many fierce &eYetis&r make their home in these frigid caves, lorded over by an &eAlpha Yeti&r.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest42.title: A Cold, Humble Abode
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest43.descr: &oFind certain structures and kill bosses? Seems easy enough... You pull out your Explorer's Compass and- wait what? It doesn't work?&r
In the Twilight Forest, you'll need to rely on your navigation skills. You'll need to rely on Twilight Forest the &eMap Focus&r!
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest43.title: Nomadic Travels
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest5.descri: The &eObsidilith&r resides in the &eObsidilith Arena&r found in &ePrimordial Pools&r in &eThe End&r.
&cThe Obsidilith spawns at minimum level of 90 and at Mythic Rarity.&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest5.title: The Galvanic Stronghold
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest6.descri: Someone already cleared out a structure before you got there? You can use &eBrimstone Nectar&r on any of the &bPrimal Titans' structures&r to reset them!
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest6.title: They're Back!
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest7.descri: As you go through Map after Map, you hear echoes of another &eLich&r... One that can be found in the &eTwilight Forest&r. A new portal appears and you step through to find a whole new dimension.

To enter the Twilight Forest, you need to create a 2x2 shallow pool of water in a grassy area, surrounded by plants and/or leaves in &eThe Otherside&r. You may need to bring grass with you. Then throw in a diamond and watch the portal form.
&l&cMobs from Twilight Forest spawn at a minimum of level 90!&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest7.title: The Unknown Beyond
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest8.descri: Nestled in small clearings throughout the &eTwilight Forest&r, &eHedge Mazes&r are a curious sight for new explorers in the dimension. Much like the ruined standing stones, monuments and other landmarks that dot the landscape, there has been endless speculation on why these mazes were built, but no answers have been found to date.

Hedge blocks form the walls of a typical maze, with dense, prickly foliage to deter adventurers from simply chopping their way through the twisting corridors. Fireflies and Jack-o-Lanterns are common, helping to light up the maze in contrast to the dim light elsewhere. Canopy Trees have been sometimes seen in a maze, forming a kind of tall post in the hedge walls.

The other main feature common to hedge mazes are the small open "rooms" within the maze. These have been found to always contain a magical spawner, as well as 1-2 chests and more Jack-o-Lanterns. Typically, they generate &eHostile Wolves, Swarm Spiders or Hedge Spiders&r but unlike spawners found elsewhere, bright light does not prevent them from functioning.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest8.title: Natural Mazes
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest9.descri: &eHostile Wolves&r are strange Wolves that can only be found in a &eHedge Maze&r. Legend says these Wolves hate humans because they always steal their treasures. Unfortunately, these Wolves cannot be tamed.
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.quest9.title: Unbridled Aggression
ftbquests.chapter.epilogue.title: Epilogue
ftbquests.chapter.exploration.quest30.de: You must discover &eall&r structures listed as dependencies to unlock this. You must then discover the &ePuzzle Dungeon&r.
ftbquests.chapter.exploration.quest41.de: Welcome to Small Ships! Small Ships expands naval travel by adding new types of ships and functionality!
Press "K" to open/close the Ships Sail.
Press "W" to increase and "S" to decrease the Sail.
To dismount an entity from the ship, right-click the entity.
To open the Inventory outside of the ship, use crouch + right-click on the ship.

To add a cannon on your ship you need to right-click the ship with a cannon in your hand. To shoot the cannon, you need to look in the direction of the cannon and press "space", while sitting in the ship. Note that you need to have a round shot in the ships inventory. To remove a cannon, right-click the ship with a pickaxe.
ftbquests.chapter.exploration.quest42.de: There are 4 different types of craftable ships: Cog, Brigg, Galley, Drakkar.

&eShip Performance&r: In terms of ship performance, cold-water ships will be 10%% faster in cold water biomes but 10%% slower in warm water biomes. Conversely, warm-water ships will be 10%% faster in warm water biomes but 10%% slower in cold water biomes. It's important to note that the speed of the ships will also be affected by the number of cannons on board, reducing speed by 2.5%% per cannon. Additionally, cargo on board can decrease the ship's speed by up to 10%%.
&eSail Coloring&r: You can customize sails by coloring them with dye items. Simply right-click the ship with the dye in your hand.
&eShip Repairing&r: You can repair your ship with iron nuggets and planks. Simply right-click the ship with the iron nuggets in your hand, you need to have a good supply of planks in your inventory.
&eShip Naming&r: You can name your ship with a name-tag. Simply right-click the ship with the name-tag in your hand.
ftbquests.chapter.exploration.title: Exploration
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest46.descri: Spawns really deep in &eDeep Ocean&r biomes.
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest47.descri: &eAquaculture&r heavily expands fishing by allowing you to catch many &enew fish, treasures&r, and customize new &etiered fishing rods&r with the &eTackle Box.&r
Fish up a &eNeptune's Bounty&r for a chance to get &eNeptunium Ingots&r which are can create powerful &eequipment.&r
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest47.title: Aquaculture
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest48.title: Gotta Catch 'em All
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest56.descri: Different Bobbers can help with fishing!
The Cork Bobber increases the size of your 'fishing bar', making catching fish easier!
The Trap Bobber causes fish to escape slower when you aren't reeling them in!
The Sonar Bobber shows what fish is on your line before it's caught!
The Treasure Bobber keeps fish from escaping when collecting Treasure Chests, and increases the chances that Treasures will appear!
The Quality Bobber greatly increases Experience gained from fishing!
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest56.title: Fishing Improvements
ftbquests.chapter.fishing.quest57.descri: Legendary Fish are exceptionally hard to catch, but grant coins and additional Fishing experience.
